data.title: ProVen Growth and Income VCT Expands Share Capital With New Admissions to LSE
data.description:
Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

Proven Growth and Income VCT ( (GB:PGOO) ) has shared an announcement.

ProVen Growth and Income VCT plc has admitted further tranches of ordinary shares to trading on the London Stock Exchange’s Main Market, following recent equity issues under its joint offer for subscription with ProVen VCT plc. New admissions totalled over 7.5 million ordinary shares across March, April and May 2026, fully fungible with existing stock and lifting the company’s total shares in issue to 335,180,416.

The staged admissions, made under a prospectus first published in November 2025, expand the VCT’s capital base and reflect ongoing investor demand for its shares. The enlarged equity pool should give the trust additional firepower for new and follow-on investments in its portfolio companies, while marginally diluting existing holdings but potentially supporting liquidity in the secondary market.

More about Proven Growth and Income VCT

ProVen Growth and Income VCT plc is a UK-listed venture capital trust investing primarily in growth-stage unquoted and AIM-quoted companies. The trust focuses on providing investors with a combination of income and capital growth by backing smaller, high-potential businesses under the UK’s VCT tax-efficient investment framework.

Average Trading Volume: 5,251

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

An announcement from 3i Group plc ( (GB:III) ) is now available.

3i Group plc has updated the market on its share capital and voting rights following the allotment of new shares under its Share Incentive Plan, bringing the total number of issued ordinary shares with voting rights to 1,021,851,960 and confirming that none are held in treasury. The company reiterated that this total voting rights figure should be used by shareholders and other obliged parties as the denominator for calculating disclosure thresholds under the FCA’s transparency rules, with the United Kingdom designated as its Home Member State for regulatory purposes.

This adjustment in total voting rights for 3i Group sets a new baseline for investors to assess significant holdings and reporting duties, helping maintain compliance with disclosure regulations and ensuring transparency in the company’s ownership structure. By clarifying its regulatory status and current share count, 3i Group supports clearer governance oversight and facilitates accurate reporting by stakeholders who monitor or influence its corporate control dynamics.

data.title: Pfizer’s VESPER-4 Obesity Trial Signals a Serious Push Into the GLP-1 Growth Market
data.description:
Pfizer Inc (PFE) announced an update on their ongoing clinical study.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

Pfizer Inc. (PFE) is advancing a late-stage obesity program with its Phase 3 VESPER-4 trial, officially titled “Evaluating The Efficacy and Safety of MET097, a Fully-Biased, Ultra Long-Acting GLP-1RA, In People With Overweight or Obesity: A Phase 3, Multi-Center Randomized, Controlled Trial (VESPER-4).” The study aims to test weekly MET097 injections for weight loss and safety in adults with overweight or obesity, underscoring Pfizer’s push to secure a share of the fast-growing obesity drug market.

The trial evaluates MET097, also known as PF-08653944, a once-weekly injectable drug designed to act on weight-control pathways similar to other GLP-1 therapies. It compares three different doses of MET097 against a placebo injection to see how much weight participants lose and how well they tolerate the treatment over time.

This is an interventional Phase 3 study with participants randomly assigned to one of three MET097 dose groups or a placebo group, in a parallel design. Both patients and trial doctors are blinded to treatment assignment, and the main goal is to measure weight loss benefits while monitoring side effects compared with placebo.

The study is listed as recruiting and is planned to run for 84 weeks, with the primary outcome measured after 64 weeks of treatment to capture meaningful weight change. Key regulatory dates include an initial submission dated 12/29/2025 and the most recent update on 05/26/2026, which signals ongoing operational activity and confirms the program remains on track.

For investors, this update reinforces Pfizer’s strategic move into the obesity space currently dominated by Novo Nordisk and Eli Lilly, where successful products have driven strong stock performance and premium valuations. Positive Phase 3 data could support a new revenue pillar for PFE, narrow the innovation gap with GLP-1 leaders, and improve sentiment around Pfizer’s long-term growth and pipeline diversification.

The VESPER-4 study of MET097 in overweight and obesity remains active and updated, with further details available on the ClinicalTrials portal.

To learn more about PFE’s potential, visit the Pfizer Inc drug pipeline page.

An announcement from Polar Capital Holdings ( (GB:POLR) ) is now available.

Polar Capital Holdings has continued to execute its previously announced share buyback programme, repurchasing 4,383 ordinary shares on 29 May 2026 via Deutsche Bank’s Deutsche Numis arm at prices between 818p and 828p. The shares will be cancelled, reducing the company’s issued ordinary share capital to 100,247,060 and confirming the same number of total voting rights, a figure shareholders can now use as a reference for disclosure thresholds under UK transparency rules.

The latest tranche of buybacks marginally tightens Polar Capital’s share base, which can enhance earnings per share and signal management’s confidence in the company’s valuation. By clarifying the updated voting rights, the group also provides greater certainty for investors and regulatory compliance, underscoring its ongoing capital management discipline amid the current market environment.

data.title: Arion Bank Launches Inflation-Linked Covered Bond Auction and Exchange Offer
data.description:
Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

An announcement from Arion Banki hf Shs Swedish DR ( (SE:ARION) ) is now available.

Arion Bank plans to issue new ISK-denominated, inflation-linked covered bonds in its ARION CBI 31 series, carrying a fixed 3.65% coupon and maturing in August 2031. The bonds will be sold via a single-price auction on 1 June 2026, with the bank retaining discretion to accept or reject offers in whole or in part.

In parallel, the bank will run an exchange offer allowing holders of the shorter-dated ARION CBI 26 bonds to swap into the new series at a clean price of 100, with settlement expected on 8 June 2026. The move supports Arion Bank’s ongoing covered bond funding programme and offers investors a way to extend duration within the bank’s inflation-linked bond structure while using either cash or existing CBI 26 bonds as payment.

More about Arion Banki hf Shs Swedish DR

Arion Bank hf. is an Icelandic financial institution that provides banking and capital markets services, including funding through covered bond issuance. The bank focuses on the domestic ISK market and uses inflation-linked covered bonds as part of its long-term funding and balance sheet management strategy.

Average Trading Volume: 49,707

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: SEK19.72B

data.title: IMI appoints Deutsche Numis to oversee second £250m share buyback tranche
data.description:

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

IMI plc ( (GB:IMI) ) has issued an announcement.

IMI plc has appointed Deutsche Bank AG’s London branch, trading as Deutsche Numis, to manage the second £250 million tranche of its previously announced £500 million share buyback programme. The second tranche will begin once the initial £250 million phase is completed and is expected to be concluded by 31 December 2026.

The buyback, limited to nearly 10% of IMI’s issued share capital excluding treasury shares, will see Deutsche Numis acquire ordinary shares on a riskless principal basis for subsequent repurchase by the company. The programme is being conducted under existing shareholder authority and in compliance with UK Listing Rules and applicable market abuse regulations, underscoring IMI’s ongoing capital return strategy to shareholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:IMI) stock is a Buy with a £3080.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on IMI plc stock, see the GB:IMI Stock Forecast page.

To see Spark’s full report on IMI stock, click here.

More about IMI plc

IMI plc is a UK-based industrial engineering group that designs and manufactures specialist flow control and motion control products for a range of end markets, including energy, transportation, and industrial automation. The company focuses on engineered solutions that improve efficiency, safety, and sustainability for customers worldwide, and its shares are listed on the London Stock Exchange.

data.title: Playtech CFO Exercises Share Options and Retains Full Allotment
data.description:

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

The latest update is out from Playtech ( (GB:PTEC) ).

Playtech has reported a share transaction involving Chief Financial Officer Chris McGinnis, who exercised options over 90,988 ordinary shares under the company’s 2022 Long Term Incentive Plan. The options were nil-cost, and the transaction was conducted outside a trading venue in line with UK Market Abuse Regulation disclosure requirements.

McGinnis plans to cover all tax liabilities arising from the option exercise using his personal funds, meaning he will retain the full allotment of shares rather than selling any portion to meet tax obligations. The move underscores executive alignment with shareholders by increasing the CFO’s direct equity stake in the business while maintaining transparency around senior management dealings in Playtech stock.

Playtech plc is a leading global B2B technology provider to the online betting and gaming industry, founded in 1999 and listed on the Main Market of the London Stock Exchange.
